description: Expert-guess noise for elite curling athletes. Speed: ~1.5% of typical delivery (~0.02 m/s). Angle: ~0.8 deg (~0.014 rad). Spin: ~5% of typical range (~0.15 rad/s). Y0: ~8mm release offset. All Gaussian.

rationale.speed: Elite curlers deliver draws at ~1.1 m/s. 0.020 m/s std means 68% of deliveries within ±2cm/s of intended speed, ~1.8% CV. Takeouts at ~2.2 m/s give ~0.9% CV.
rationale.angle: 0.014 rad = 0.8 deg. At 6.4m hog-to-tee, this is ±9cm lateral error at the tee line from aim alone, consistent with competitive accuracy.
rationale.spin: Typical rotation 0.5-2.0 rad/s. 0.15 std means ±10% variation in curl trajectory.
rationale.y0: 8mm std in lateral release position. Elite athletes have very consistent release mechanics.
